hero_title: Market Visibility for Digital Asset Supervision
hero_subtitle: SFTY supports government teams by connecting fragmented data sources into one secure supervisory operating layer for clearer market view and risk-based oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Supervisory visibility weakens without market context
problem_description: Regulators struggle to build a clear view when data on VASPs, wallets and transactions remains isolated across systems. This limits risk-based oversight, delays case development and complicates defensible supervisory decisions.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects VASP profiles, wallet and transaction data, risk signals, cases and supervisory actions into one secure operating layer for clearer and more efficient oversight of the digital asset market.
workflow_title: From market signal to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY collects market signals from multiple data sources and routes them through alerts, cases, investigations and reports for structured supervisory decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. A supervisory operating layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY helps regulators unify fragmented market data, entity context, cases and evidence into one secure platform for clearer digital asset market visibility and oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY supports government authorities with secure deployment, role-based access and evidence-grade logging essential for governed, audit-ready supervisory market visibility.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY improve market visibility for regulators
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how supervisory workflows connect fragmented market data
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports regulatory case review
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for government supervisory teams
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for regulators and authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a supervisory operating layer connecting VASP and CASP data, licensing, transactions, cases and reporting into one secure environment for regulators and government authorities.
faq_2_question: How can SFTY support market-level supervision?
faq_2_answer: SFTY consolidates fragmented signals across wallets, entities, licenses and transactions to deliver structured, risk-based supervisory workflows and evidence-ready material for ongoing market oversight.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and linked evidence in audit-grade records, supporting defensible supervisory reviews and reporting workflows workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple data providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY orchestrates multiple KYT, KYC, KYB, Travel Rule and blockchain analytics providers into connected, normalized data flows for comprehensive supervisory insight.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing tools, connecting their outputs into structured supervisory cases and reports without replacing underlying analytics or compliance providers.
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects regulated entity data, wallet activity, risk signals and cases into a structured supervisory environment.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
